summary: The Bermond-Thomassen conjecture ($\delta^+(D) \geq 2k-1 \Rightarrow k$ disjoint cycles) remains open for general digraphs with $k \geq 4$. Significant post-2007 advances include a new shorter proof for $k=3$ (Bai and Manoussakis, 2018) and verification for triangle-free multipartite and 3-partite tournaments (Gutin et al., 2023). Notably, the stronger conjecture of Bang-Jensen, Bessy, and Thomassé on girth conditions (listed in the OPG discussion) was disproved by Bai and Manoussakis (2018).

since_posted.title: On the number of vertex-disjoint cycles in digraphs
since_posted.claim: Provides a new shorter proof of the Bermond-Thomassen conjecture for $k=3$, and disproves the stronger Bang-Jensen--Bessy--Thomassé conjecture on girth-conditioned vertex-disjoint cycles.

since_posted.title: Two disjoint cycles in digraphs
since_posted.authors: Mikołaj Lewandowski, Joanna Polcyn, Christian Reiher
since_posted.claim: Characterises all outdegree sequences forcing $k$ vertex-disjoint cycles for $k \leq 2$, giving a complete answer for the two-cycle case that strengthens the Bermond-Thomassen bound.

since_posted.title: Note on Disjoint Cycles in Multipartite Tournaments
since_posted.authors: Gregory Gutin, Wei Li, Shujing Wang, Anders Yeo, Yacong Zhou
since_posted.claim: Verifies the Bermond-Thomassen conjecture for the special classes of triangle-free multipartite tournaments and 3-partite tournaments.

since_posted.title: Vertex-disjoint cycles of different lengths in tournaments
since_posted.claim: Proves that every tournament with minimum outdegree $\geq 2k-1$ ($k \geq 5$) contains $k$ disjoint cycles with at least three of different lengths, a variant of the Bermond-Thomassen problem in the tournament setting.

search_queries: Bermond-Thomassen conjecture disjoint cycles digraph minimum out-degree 2k-1 proof
Bermond Thomassen conjecture disjoint cycles digraphs recent progress arXiv
disjoint cycles digraph minimum out-degree conjecture Lichiardopol Bessy Thomasse 2008 2009 2010
notes: The conjecture is open for $k \geq 4$ in general digraphs. The Bang-Jensen--Bessy--Thomassé stronger conjecture (mentioned in the OPG discussion) was disproved by Bai--Manoussakis 2018. Publication venue details for arXiv papers could not be verified from fetches alone; all are listed as preprints. The 2022 Lewandowski--Polcyn--Reiher and 2023 Gutin et al. papers may have appeared in journals by now but this was not confirmed.
